# Roblox Template Instructions

This repository is a Roblox game template built around Rojo, Rokit, Luau, and a generated `default.project.json`.

## Core workflow

- Treat `default.project.json` as generated output. Do not edit it manually unless the user explicitly asks for that exact file.
- The source of truth for Rojo tree generation is `tools/genRojoTree.js`.
- Before making changes that affect project structure under `src/`, assume the `Watch Rojo Tree` VS Code task should be running so `default.project.json` stays in sync.
- If a task or script touches the Rojo tree, prefer updating `tools/genRojoTree.js`, `package.json`, or `.vscode/tasks.json` instead of editing generated JSON.

## genRojoTree conventions

- The repository intentionally keeps a compact VS Code layout and maps files to Roblox services through `tools/genRojoTree.js`.
- `Client.luau` and `Utils.luau` are expected to end up in `ReplicatedStorage`.
- `Server.luau` is expected to end up in `ServerScriptService`.
- `src/Startup/Client.client.luau` maps to `StarterPlayer/StarterPlayerScripts`.
- `src/Startup/Server.server.luau` maps to `ServerScriptService`.
- `src/UI` and `src/Startup` are handled explicitly in the generated tree and are blacklisted from the recursive generic scan inside `tools/genRojoTree.js`.
- `init.luau` claims its parent folder in the generated tree; when present, child files under that claimed folder are not mapped individually.

## Expected project layout

- Main gameplay code lives under `src/Classes`, `src/Modules`, and `src/Services`.
- Services commonly use a compact folder layout such as `<ServiceName>/Client.luau`, `<ServiceName>/Server.luau`, and optional `<ServiceName>/Utils.luau`.
- UI code lives under `src/UI`.
- Startup entrypoints live under `src/Startup`.

## VS Code workspace expectations

- Preserve `.vscode/settings.json` custom editor labels that make `Client.luau`, `Server.luau`, and `Utils.luau` readable in tabs.
- Preserve `.vscode/tasks.json` task `Watch Rojo Tree`, which runs `npm run watch:rojo`.

## Build and validation guidance

- Install Node dependencies before relying on the watch task or Rojo tree generation.
- Use `npm run build:rojo` to regenerate `default.project.json` once.
- Use `npm run watch:rojo` or the `Watch Rojo Tree` VS Code task to keep the file updated while developing.
- When testing structural changes, verify both `tools/genRojoTree.js` and the generated `default.project.json`.

## Known repository-specific pitfalls

- If `tools/genRojoTree.js` is reused for another game, update `tree.name` to the actual project name.
- If a `CustomPackages` entry exists under `tree.ReplicatedStorage` for a future customization, remove it when it is not needed.
- If `sourcemap.json` becomes stale or broken, it can be deleted and regenerated by restarting VS Code so Luau Language Server recreates it.

## Documentation

- Prefer these local docs before guessing workflow details: `Readmes/genrojotree-setup.md`, `Readmes/README.md`, `Readmes/networker.md`, and `Readmes/testez.md`.
